units.content: There is a software architecture with three components: Opus (orchestrator), Haiku (extraction instrument), describe.py (measurement framework)
A core mechanism exists with 5 steps: extract unconditioned, extract conditioned, blend using guidance formula, check stability, increase resolution if unstable
The approach implements classifier-free guidance for information extraction
The code defines an ExtractionSchema class that constrains extraction without determining output
ExtractionSchema includes predicate categories: see, hear, feel, touch, think, know, want, believe, say
Function _call_haiku makes API calls to Claude Haiku with text and system prompts
Function extract_unconditioned extracts predicates from text alone, without context anchors
Function extract_conditioned extracts predicates from text with numerical context anchors
The context anchors constrain extraction without determining it, serving as a conditioning mechanism
Function blend_extractions applies the formula: guided = unconditioned + guidance_scale × (conditioned - unconditioned)
At guidance_scale=0, text dominates; at scale=1, context dominates; intermediate values blend both
Function run_denoising_loop iterates through resolutions: document, paragraph, sentence
The loop re-runs extraction N times to compute variance across re-runs for each property
If variance is below convergence_threshold, the extraction has stabilized
Low variance across re-runs causes the loop to converge and stop iterating
High variance causes the loop to increase resolution (finer grain segmentation) and repeat
The depth at which convergence occurs serves as a measurement of appropriate resolution for the text
